steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The question asks to emphasize a difference between Bengali and Danish nouns. The focus must be on directly contrasting how nouns function in each language, not on language families or other properties.

steps.title: Find the choice that directly contrasts noun behavior
steps.content:
Choice A directly compares how nouns work: Bengali appends a suffix to the noun to indicate location, while Danish leaves the noun unchanged and uses a preposition instead.

The other three options either focus on language families, mention noun change but shift to language families, or explain noun behavior as a consequence of language family rather than as a direct contrast of how nouns themselves are used.

This direct parallel comparison of noun functions accomplishes exactly what the goal requires.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
